signals.headline: Fed Rate Cuts Delayed as Inflation Accelerates on Energy Shock | YES | HIGH confidence**
signals.raw:
Fed Rate Cuts Delayed as Inflation Accelerates on Energy Shock | YES | HIGH confidence**

The Federal Reserve is holding its benchmark rate at 3.50%–3.75% as inflation accelerates, with most policymakers now expecting at most one rate cut in 2026, and a growing minority seeing no cuts at all.[2] This represents a significant shift from earlier expectations.
